title: Folding Table Hinge — Self-Locking and Motorised Actuation
content: A self-folding table uses motorised hinge actuators (servo or DC gear motor with lead screw) to fold/unfold table leaves. Self-locking requires either a worm gear (gear ratio > 20:1 provides back-drive protection) or an over-centre locking hinge.
extended_content: Actuation options: 1) Servo (HS-755HB, 35 kg·cm) per hinge — direct PWM control. 2) DC gear motor + limit switches — position feedback via microswitch. 3) Linear actuator (12 V, 10 mm/s, 500 N) — push/pull table leaf via push-rod linkage. Table leaf mass: 2–5 kg; torque at hinge T = m·g·L_cm = 3 × 9.81 × 0.3 = 8.8 N·m per leaf (L_cm = distance to CG). Safety: soft-start PWM to prevent impact; obstacle detection via current sensing (stall detection). Fold/unfold time: 3–8 s. MCU trigger: IR sensor detects user approach; voice command; button. Stored position memory with EEPROM.
formula: T_hinge = m·g·L_cm ;  worm gear back-drive prevention: ratio ≥ 20:1
units: T: N·m, m: kg, L: m
source: Shigley's Mechanical Engineering Design, 10th ed.; Servo City Technical Notes; Parker Hannifin Linear Actuator Selection Guide
